faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Frequently asked questions
faq.helpText: Can't find the answer to your question?
faq.orReport: or report an issue on GitHub.
faq.question: question

app-data-privacy.a: Yes, all your data is stored only on your device (IndexedDB). Nothing is sent to a server. You have full control over your personal information. Remember to make regular backups via the Export function in settings.

app-offline.a: Yes! Chrysalide is a PWA (Progressive Web App) that works entirely offline once installed. All features are available without an internet connection.

app-backup.q: How do I back up my data?
app-backup.a: Go to Settings > Export data. A JSON file containing all your data will be downloaded. Keep it in a safe place. To restore, use the Import function in settings.

app-install.q: How do I install the app on my phone?
app-install.a: On Android (Chrome): Menu ⋮ > "Install app" or "Add to home screen". On iOS (Safari): Share button > "Add to Home Screen". The app will be available like a native application.

app-notifications.q: How do I enable medication reminders?
app-notifications.a: Go to Settings > Notifications and enable reminders. Make sure to allow notifications in your browser. Reminders work even when the app is closed (if installed as a PWA).

app-delete-data.q: How do I delete all my data?
app-delete-data.a: Go to Settings > Delete all data. This action is irreversible. Consider exporting your data first if you want to keep it.

med-ranges.q: What do the reference ranges on the charts mean?
med-ranges.a: The colored zones on the test charts represent target ranges for HRT. Values in the green zone are generally optimal. These ranges are indicative — only your doctor can interpret your results in your personal context.

med-tracking.a: Tracking helps you: 1) Not miss doses, 2) Have an accurate history for medical appointments, 3) Identify patterns (recurring missed doses, side effects), 4) Manage your stock and anticipate renewals.

med-not-advice.q: Does this app replace medical supervision?
med-not-advice.a: No, absolutely not. Chrysalide is a personal tracking tool, not medical advice. Always consult a healthcare professional for your medical decisions. The app helps you organize your information for appointments.

gen-name.a: The chrysalis is the cocoon in which a caterpillar transforms into a butterfly. It's a metaphor for transition: a protected space for personal transformation. The butterfly that emerges symbolizes flourishing.

gen-contribute.q: How can I contribute to the project?
gen-contribute.a: Chrysalide is an open source project. You can contribute via GitHub: report bugs, suggest features, improve code or documentation. All feedback is valuable!

gen-support.q: I need urgent help, what should I do?
gen-support.a: If you're in distress, contact a helpline (see Resources > Support). In case of medical emergency, call your local emergency number (911 in the US, 112 in Europe). You are not alone.
